definition:
The "inverted ground truth corpus" is a bottom-up notion of truth: the corpus IS the ground truth, accreted row-by-row from real, signed-off execution outcomes, rather than a top-down, hand-authored "gold" label set written in advance. "Inverted" means outcomes-define-truth: a (fault-signature -> plan -> outcome) triple becomes admissible knowledge only AFTER the plan was actually executed, the outcome was verified against the original failure signature, and a verifier or human signed it off. Truth is therefore EARNED at the end of the pipeline (the sign-off gate is the truth-admission boundary), not asserted at the start.

Two reinforcing senses of "inverted" are present in the code: (1) outcomes-define-truth (the dominant sense) -- the engine ships NO corpus and starts empty at cold start, so every true row is grown from a confirmed outcome, the inverse of seeding a labeled gold set up front; and (2) failures-are-kept-as-hard-negatives -- a failed/escalated/unresolved ticket is NOT discarded but admitted to the corpus as a labeled hard negative, so the corpus encodes "what did NOT work for this signature" as first-class truth, never offered as a fix. The CEC-Platform bake-off harness shows the same inversion in its eval design: "Gold answers are computed, not authored" -- gold is captured by executing/verifying, not declared, and the contract treats synthesis on a no-conclusion trace as a zero-tolerance failure (a retracted claim must not become "truth").

Note: the literal string "inverted ground truth corpus" is the orchestrating workflow's conceptual LABEL (it appears only in /home/nathan/CEC_AutoDiagnoser/.claude/wf-recon.js, the recon script that spawned this task), not a verbatim string in the engine or platform source. The concept it names, however, is fully implemented in crates/corpus-client and described across the README and corpus/SCHEMA.md.
how_it_works:
Mechanically, the corpus is grown at the END of the diagnostic pipeline, gated, and read back FIRST on the next run:

1. PIPELINE TO TRUTH. The flow is: request -> intake -> diagnostics -> candidate plans -> judge panel -> execute winning plan (consent-gated) -> verify outcome -> sign-off -> write to corpus (de-identified) (README "support request -> ... -> verify outcome -> sign-off -> write to corpus"). Nothing is written unless --sign-off ran: it executes the winner, "verifies the outcome by diffing a re-collected signature against the original failure signature," emits an OutcomeLabel, and records the de-identified triple through the gate.

2. SIGN-OFF GATE = TRUTH-ADMISSION BOUNDARY (invariant 5/6). SignOff is an enum {Unconfirmed, VerifierConfirmed, HumanConfirmed} (schema.rs). ensure_signed_off (gate.rs) returns GateError unless sign_off.is_confirmed(). EVERY CorpusStore::submit impl (LocalCorpus, FileCorpus, HttpCorpus in store.rs) calls ensure_signed_off BEFORE any state change -- tests assert an unconfirmed row never reaches memory, disk, or the network ("an unconfirmed contribution must never reach disk"). So a row exists in the corpus only because it was confirmed; the gate is the single admission point for truth.

3. LABELING IS MANDATORY; FAILURES ARE TRUTH TOO. Sign-off ALWAYS emits a label, including for unresolved/hardware-escalated tickets, "because an unlabeled ticket is corpus poison. A corpus of plans without disciplined outcomes is a pile of plausible scripts" (schema.rs). OutcomeLabel::is_resolved() distinguishes fixes from hard negatives; fix_mappings (store.rs) backs a FixMapping only from resolved rows, while failures "stay in the rows as hard negatives" and are "not offered as a fix" (test: hard_negatives_are_stored_but_not_retrieved_as_fixes).

4. RETRIEVAL-FIRST FLYWHEEL. With --corpus <PATH>, FileCorpus persists "one JSON row per line, gate-checked before anything touches disk." The next run facing a known signature at the same ConfigClass is served retrieval-first: confirmed precedents join the slate as CorpusPrimed candidates the judge prefers, "de novo model generation is skipped, and hard negatives stay in the rows without ever being offered as fixes" (README "The flywheel"). Retrieval is scoped to a matching ConfigClass (test: retrieval_is_scoped_to_the_config_class), and repeated confirmations of the same plan aggregate into one mapping with a confirmation count (confirmations: u32; test: confirmations_aggregate_per_plan) -- truth strengthens by accretion of independent confirmed outcomes.

5. DE-IDENTIFICATION BY STRUCTURED EXTRACTION (not scrubbing). A row "never carries free text." FaultSignature is built from a fixed vocabulary via common::extract_symptoms (only fault terms, 0x-hex codes, id-bearing tokens like event_41/xid_79, bare module names survive). Contribution::new de-identifies the plan via de_identify_plan, replacing every free-text step description and the title with the action vocabulary (e.g. "cim_query -> registry_set"). An adversarial leakage suite (leakage_tests in lib.rs) seeds known identifiers (hostnames, usernames, paths, MAC, serial, email, IP) through the whole path and asserts ZERO leakage into the serialized row, while still asserting the structured evidence (explorer.exe, 0x1234, driver_rollback) survives.

6. PLATFORM-SIDE PARALLEL (CEC-Platform corpus). corpus/SCHEMA.md implements the same inversion at a larger scale for the PCB platform: two zones (staging = agent-writable, advisory-only, can never block; promoted = human-only behind CODEOWNERS + branch protection, the only zone that compiles into blocking artifacts). Promotion is the truth-admission act ("status: promoted" + complete signoff block + residence in corpus/promoted/). Provenance is mandatory and "model output is NOT a source"; the bake-off methodology mirrors it: "Gold answers are computed, not authored ... contamination-resistant" (cec_reasoning_bakeoff.py). The Monotone-tightening law (EI-07) formalizes that unsigned influence may only make evaluation STRICTER, and unsigned generation moves are rolled back unless they beat a control -- truth never loosens without an owner signature.
contrast_with_normal_ground_truth:
CONVENTIONAL / TOP-DOWN GROUND TRUTH: a fixed "gold" label set is authored up front by annotators, frozen, and the system is measured against it. Truth precedes the system; it is a static answer key; failures/negatives are usually excluded; correctness is "does the output match the pre-written label." Provenance lives in the annotation process, once.

INVERTED / BOTTOM-UP GROUND TRUTH (this project): there is no pre-authored key. The engine SHIPS NO CORPUS and starts empty (invariant 1/2, "cold start"). Each true row is grown at the end of a real run from a verified, signed-off outcome, so truth is the ACCUMULATED, ever-growing set of confirmed outcomes, keyed by (FaultSignature, ConfigClass). Differences:
- Direction: truth is produced by the system's own verified outcomes (bottom-up) instead of supplied to it (top-down).
- Failures: hard negatives are first-class admitted truth ("a failure is a hard negative, not a discard"), whereas a gold set typically holds only positives.
- Admission: a sign-off gate / human-promotion gate admits truth at the END (ensure_signed_off; promoted-zone CODEOWNERS), versus annotation at the START.
- Growth + strengthening: the set grows monotonically and individual mappings strengthen via confirmation counts; a gold set is fixed.
- Contamination resistance: because truth is computed/verified per run (and bake-off gold is "computed, not authored" with seeded novel values), there is no static answer key to leak or memorize. The platform even bars model output from being a source and treats synthesis on a no-conclusion/retracted trace as a zero-tolerance failure.

In short: a normal ground-truth set is a frozen answer key written before the system runs; the inverted corpus IS the answer key, written by the system's own verified, signed-off outcomes as it runs -- which is exactly why integrity of each accreted row, not of one upfront labeling pass, is the load-bearing concern.
implications_for_evidence_integrity: Per-row integrity is the whole ballgame. Because truth is accreted one signed-off row at a time (not vetted once as a frozen gold set), the integrity of EACH admitted row is load-bearing -- a single poisoned/mislabeled row becomes 'truth' the next run retrieves and prefers. A checklist must verify integrity at the granularity of the individual Contribution, not the dataset.

Sign-off is the truth-admission boundary -- audit it as such. ensure_signed_off (gate.rs) is the single choke point where outcomes become truth. The checklist must confirm every write path (LocalCorpus, FileCorpus, HttpCorpus, and any future backend) calls the gate BEFORE any state change, and that the SignOff level actually matches the consent level that authorized execution (verifier = reversible, human = destructive; hardware/ambiguous routes require human). A gate bypass = unverified data minted as ground truth.

Provenance of every claim must be intrinsic and non-model. Each row must carry what made it true: the verified outcome label, the ConfigClass it is scoped to, and (platform side) a source whose type is standard|datasheet|fab|spec|decision|measurement -- 'model output is NOT a source.' The checklist should reject any row whose 'evidence' resolves to a model artifact (CEC-Platform lint already does), and require a ledger/run-id or verification hook backing each finding.

No leakage is a continuous, adversarial requirement -- not a one-time scrub. De-identification is by structured extraction; the only guarantee that identifiers do not enter the permanent truth store is the adversarial leakage suite. The checklist must keep that suite green and EXTEND it whenever the schema, fault vocabulary, or plan shape changes -- 'a scrubbing pass that is never adversarially tested is a scrubbing pass that leaks.'

Hard negatives are truth and must be preserved and quarantined. Failures must be admitted (labeled, signed) AND must never be retrievable as a fix (only is_resolved() rows back a FixMapping). The checklist must verify the resolved/negative split holds end-to-end so a known-bad plan is never re-offered, and that no label is silently dropped (an unlabeled ticket is corpus poison).

No train/serve leakage and no contamination of the key. Since the corpus is read retrieval-first and is also the truth set, evaluation and seeding must stay separate: gold for benchmarking is 'computed, not authored' with seeded novel values for contamination resistance; never reuse burned fixtures (the bake-off explicitly avoids 'the burned CL-11 fixtures'). A checklist must prevent eval cases or model-generated content from being admitted as corpus truth.

Retrieval scoping is part of correctness. A confirmed outcome is truth ONLY within its ConfigClass; cross-class retrieval would launder a fact into a context where it was never verified. The checklist must assert config-class scoping on every query (tested) and that ConfigClass is derived honestly (BOM revision or inventory hash).

Admission must be append-only and tamper-evident. FileCorpus is append-only JSONL gate-checked before disk; plan provenance is HMAC-signed and re-verified at the executor (provenance crate). The checklist should require that accreted rows cannot be silently rewritten, that an unparseable corpus is an error not silent data loss, and that confirmation counts only increment from genuinely independent confirmed outcomes.

Promotion/owner-gating is the human truth boundary at platform scale, and truth can only tighten. On CEC-Platform, only the human-gated promoted zone yields blocking artifacts; the Monotone-tightening law (EI-07) means unsigned influence can only make the bar STRICTER and unsigned generation moves roll back unless they beat a control. A research/integrity checklist must preserve this asymmetry: never let an unsigned/agent channel loosen evaluation or self-promote an entry past its class, and treat a revoked source as poisoning every dependent row (EI-06).
verbatim_excerpts: README.md (pipeline): 'support request → intake interview (map input to a case, ask follow-ups) → collect diagnostics → generate candidate plans (swarm) → judge panel (route, score, escalate, pick best) → execute winning plan (consented) → verify outcome → sign-off → write to corpus (de-identified)'

README.md (invariant 5, Sign-off gate): 'corpus-client refuses to submit any outcome that is not verifier-confirmed or human-confirmed. This is enforced in code (ensure_signed_off), not in documentation.'

README.md (closing the loop): 'verifies the outcome by diffing a re-collected signature against the original failure signature, emits an outcome label ... and records the de-identified triple through the corpus sign-off gate'

README.md (the flywheel): 'Outcomes persist across runs, so the next run facing a known signature at the same config class is served retrieval-first: confirmed precedents join the slate as CorpusPrimed candidates (which the judge prefers under corpus priors), de novo model generation is skipped, and hard negatives stay in the rows without ever being offered as fixes.'

README.md (de-identification): 'A corpus row never carries free text. ... only a fixed fault vocabulary, 0x-hex codes, id-bearing terms (event_41, xid_79), and bare module names survive ... An adversarial leakage test suite seeds known identifiers through the whole path and asserts zero leakage into serialized corpus rows.'

schema.rs (OutcomeLabel doc): 'Sign-off is the labeling event: it always emits a label, including for unresolved and hardware-escalated tickets, because an unlabeled ticket is corpus poison. A corpus of plans without disciplined outcomes is a pile of plausible scripts.'
schema.rs (is_resolved doc): 'Every other label still enters the corpus — a failure is a hard negative, not a discard — but only resolved outcomes back a FixMapping.'

store.rs (fix_mappings doc): 'Only resolved rows back a mapping (failures stay in the rows as hard negatives); rows confirming the same plan for the same signature aggregate into one mapping with a confirmation count.'
store.rs (test assertions): '"the failure is kept as a hard negative"' and '"a failure must not be offered as a fix"' and '"an unconfirmed contribution must never reach disk"'
gate.rs: 'pub fn ensure_signed_off(contribution: &Contribution) -> Result<(), GateError> { if contribution.sign_off.is_confirmed() { Ok(()) } else { Err(GateError) } }'
lib.rs (de-identification doc): 'A corpus row is de-identified by structured extraction, not scrubbing ... a scrubbing pass that is never adversarially tested is a scrubbing pass that leaks.'

cec_reasoning_bakeoff.py (lines 13-16): 'Gold answers are computed, not authored: logic grids are brute-force verified unique, code-prediction gold is captured by exec'ing the snippet at gen time, math/domain answers come from the same formulas the prompts state. Seeded parameter draws -> novel values, contamination-resistant.'

CEC-Platform corpus/SCHEMA.md (Zones): 'staging ... ADVISORY checks only ... can never block' vs 'promoted ... human only (CODEOWNERS + branch protection: owner's GitHub-verified approval) ... blocking artifacts'
CEC-Platform corpus/SCHEMA.md (source): 'provenance is mandatory; model output is NOT a source'
CEC-Platform corpus/SCHEMA.md (EI-07): 'the unsigned loop can only ever make the bar HARDER to clear. It cannot lower the bar to let a worse board through ...'

tasks-workload-v1.json (T-104, the retraction/no-leakage-of-retracted-claims case): 'An earlier report claimed the sense divider was misvalued, but that claim was RETRACTED after re-measurement confirmed 49.9k/10k as designed. ... do not report claims the trace retracts.'
